summary: Multiple agent framework releases (Microsoft ADK Go 2.0, Microsoft agent-framework), security focus (containment, tool-scoping, permission guards), and new memory/observability tools dominate the signal landscape.

candidates.title: ADK Go 2.0: graph-based workflow engine, human-in-the-loop, multi-agent reliability
candidates.why_it_matters: Google's upgraded agent framework introduces graph-based orchestration and HITL detection.

candidates.title: Microsoft agent-framework: multi-agent orchestration with Python/.NET support
candidates.why_it_matters: Microsoft's official multi-agent framework now at 11k+ stars.

candidates.title: Anthropic: How we contain Claude across products (blast radius containment)
candidates.why_it_matters: Detailed ingestion of containment patterns offers production security design patterns.

candidates.title: OpenAI: separating signal from noise in coding evaluations
candidates.why_it_matters: Official guidance on eval rigor for coding agents.

candidates.title: Claude Sonnet 5: frontier performance for coding/agents
candidates.why_it_matters: New frontier model with direct coding/agent improvements.

candidates.title: user reports: completing Google vibe coding course with agentic engineering
candidates.why_it_matters: Community learning path for agent building practices.

candidates.title: strict tool-scoping/sandboxing required for agent memory security
candidates.why_it_matters: Security practitioner highlighting practical least-privilege architecture needs.

candidates.title: GitHub Copilot in VS Code June 2026 releases (changelog)
candidates.why_it_matters: Official product update with likely agent workflow enhancements.

candidates.title: Microsoft Flint: visualization language for AI agents
candidates.why_it_matters: New approach to understanding multi-agent workflows through visualization.

candidates.title: agent-armor: framework to detect AI Agent Traps (content injection, jailbreaks, exfiltration)
candidates.why_it_matters: Addresses emerging security taxonomies from DeepMind with practical tool.

candidates.title: stealth-chrome-devtools-mcp: undetectable browser automation via MCP
candidates.why_it_matters: Enables stealth browser control for agents; defense/red-team angle.

candidates.title: opencloudcosts MCP server: cloud pricing for AWS/GCP/Azure as agent tool
candidates.why_it_matters: New MCP primitive for cost-aware agent decisions in cloud environments.

gaps: Missing user_workflow: additional concrete field reports from operators
Missing infra_primitive: memory or storage primitives with high confidence
